key_insight: NebulaExp-8B shows that a fully transparent, ablation-driven post-training pipeline can meaningfully improve both instruction following and reasoning in an 8B LLM.
engineering_relevance: The paper provides a reproducible recipe for lightweight model post-training, useful for teams optimizing open 8B-scale models without relying on opaque data or training choices.
method: Built a two-branch post-training pipeline on Qwen3-8B-base: instruction-focused and reasoning-specialized.
Curated 3.84M SFT samples plus a 200K verifiable RL candidate pool with filtering, difficulty grading, task classification, and diversity-aware sampling.
Compared supervised fine-tuning, GRPO RL, and OPD/MOPD teacher-based distillation across ablations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains from 55.01 to 60.99 after NebulaExp-Ins-SFT and to 61.85 with GRPO, while reasoning scores rose from 73.88 to 75.17 and 10K-sample MOPD improved average performance by 4.18.
watch_item: Results depend on strong data curation and verifiers, so the pipeline may be harder to reproduce if the same filtering rules, teacher quality, or evaluation setup are unavailable.
paper_title: NebulaExp-8B: An Empirical Post-Training Pipeline via Full-Scale Ablation Research
